title: Lawful processing basis
description: A person may process personal data only for a lawful purpose with consent or for certain legitimate uses.

title: Notice before consent
description: Every request for consent must be accompanied or preceded by a notice informing the Data Principal of the personal data, purpose, rights, and complaint mechanism.

title: Notice for existing consent
description: Where consent was given before commencement, Data Fiduciary shall as soon as practicable give a notice similar to section 5(1).
deadline: As soon as reasonably practicable after commencement

title: Notice in multiple languages
description: Give option to access notice in English or any language in Eighth Schedule of Constitution.

title: Consent requirements
description: Consent must be free, specific, informed, unconditional, unambiguous, by clear affirmative action, limited to necessary personal data for specified purpose.

title: Consent request language and details
description: Consent request must be in clear and plain language, with option for English or Eighth Schedule language, and provide contact of DPO or authorized person.

title: Right to withdraw consent
description: Data Principal may withdraw consent at any time with ease comparable to giving consent.
notes: Data Fiduciary must enable withdrawal easily.

title: Cease processing upon withdrawal
description: Upon withdrawal of consent, Data Fiduciary shall within reasonable time cease and cause Data Processors to cease processing unless required/authorized by law.

title: Consent Manager facilitation
description: Data Principal may give/manage/review/withdraw consent through a Consent Manager. Consent Manager must be registered with Board.
notes: Further rules in Rule 4 and First Schedule.

title: Burden of proof for consent
description: In a proceeding, Data Fiduciary must prove notice was given and consent was obtained in accordance with Act.

title: Data Fiduciary responsibility for compliance
description: Data Fiduciary is responsible for compliance of itself and its Data Processors.

title: Valid contract with Data Processor
description: Engagement of Data Processor must be under a valid contract.

title: Accuracy, completeness, consistency
description: Where personal data is likely used to make a decision affecting Data Principal or disclosed to another Data Fiduciary, ensure its completeness, accuracy, consistency.

title: Technical and organizational measures
description: Implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure effective observance of Act and rules.

title: Security safeguards to prevent breach
description: Protect personal data by taking reasonable security safeguards to prevent personal data breach.

title: Intimate personal data breach
description: In case of a personal data breach, give intimation to Board and each affected Data Principal in prescribed form and manner.
deadline: Without delay (to Board with detailed report within 72 hours)

title: Erasure of personal data
description: Erase personal data upon withdrawal of consent or when specified purpose is no longer served, whichever earlier; unless retention required by law. Also cause Data Processor to erase.

title: Publish contact information
description: Publish business contact information of DPO or person able to answer questions about processing.

title: Grievance redressal mechanism
description: Establish an effective mechanism to redress grievances of Data Principals.

title: Verifiable consent for child's data
description: Obtain verifiable consent of parent before processing any personal data of a child.
notes: Detailed in Rule 10. 'Parent' includes lawful guardian.

title: No detrimental processing of child's data
description: Not undertake processing likely to cause detrimental effect on well-being of a child.

title: Prohibition on tracking and targeted advertising to children
description: Not undertake tracking or behavioural monitoring of children or targeted advertising directed at children.

title: Appoint Data Protection Officer (Significant Data Fiduciary)
description: Appoint a Data Protection Officer based in India, responsible to board, and point of contact for grievance redressal.
deadline: Upon notification as Significant Data Fiduciary

title: Appoint independent data auditor
description: Appoint an independent data auditor to carry out data audit evaluating compliance.
deadline: Upon notification as Significant Data Fiduciary

title: Periodic Data Protection Impact Assessment
description: Undertake periodic Data Protection Impact Assessment.
deadline: At least once every 12 months (per Rule 13)

title: Periodic audit
description: Undertake periodic audit.
deadline: At least once every 12 months (per Rule 13)

title: Right to nominate
description: Allow Data Principal to nominate an individual to exercise rights upon death or incapacity.

title: Data Principal duties
description: Data Principal must comply with laws, not impersonate, not suppress material info, not file frivolous complaints, furnish authentic info for correction/erasure.

title: Notice presentation requirements
description: Notice must be presented independently, in clear and plain language, include itemized personal data description and specified purpose, withdrawal means, rights, complaint mechanism.

title: Consent Manager registration and obligations
description: Consent Manager must register with Board, fulfill conditions in First Schedule Part A, and comply with obligations in Part B (e.g., record maintenance, security, no conflict of interest).
deadline: Rule 4 comes into force 1 year after publication
penalty_ref: Schedule - Items 2,7 (breach of obligations can lead to penalty)
notes: Detailed obligations in First Schedule Part B.

title: State processing for subsidies etc. - follow standards
description: Processing of personal data for provision of subsidy, benefit, service, certificate, licence, or permit by State must follow Second Schedule standards.

title: Reasonable security safeguards (detailed)
description: Protect personal data by: encryption/obfuscation/masking, access control, logs and monitoring, data backups, retain logs and personal data for 1 year, contract provisions with Data Processor, technical and organizational measures.
notes: Log retention period: at least 1 year (Rule 6(1)(e)).

title: Breach intimation to Data Principal
description: On becoming aware of breach, without delay intimate each affected Data Principal via user account or registered contact, providing description, consequences, measures taken, safety measures, contact person.

title: Breach intimation to Board
description: Intimate to Board: (a) without delay: description, nature, extent, timing, location, likely impact; (b) within 72 hours or longer if allowed: updated detailed info, facts, circumstances, reasons, measures, findings, remedial measures, report of intimations to Data Principals.
deadline: Immediate initial intimation; detailed report within 72 hours
notes: Board may allow longer period if written request.

title: Erasure after specified time period (Third Schedule)
description: Data Fiduciaries of classes specified in Third Schedule shall erase personal data after the corresponding time period if Data Principal does not approach or exercise rights, unless retention required by law.
applies_to: Data Fiduciary (specified classes: e-commerce ≥2 crore users, online gaming ≥50 lakh users, social media ≥2 crore users)
deadline: Upon expiry of time period in Third Schedule (3 years)
notes: 48 hours notice before erasure per Rule 8(2).

title: Notice before erasure
description: At least 48 hours before erasure under Rule 8(1), inform Data Principal that personal data will be erased unless she logs in or initiates contact.

title: Minimum retention of logs for 1 year
description: Retain personal data, associated traffic data, and logs for minimum 1 year from date of processing for purposes in Seventh Schedule, then erase unless longer retention required by law.
deadline: Upon enforcement (but from date of processing, retention for at least 1 year)

title: Publish contact information for questions
description: Prominently publish on website/app the business contact information of DPO or person able to answer questions about processing; also mention in response to rights exercise communications.

title: Verifiable consent for child - due diligence
description: Adopt technical and organizational measures to ensure verifiable consent of parent; observe due diligence by checking identity and age via reliable details available or voluntarily provided through authorized entity or virtual token.
notes: Adult defined as 18+; authorized entity includes Digital Locker service provider.

title: Verifiable consent for person with disability
description: When obtaining verifiable consent from lawful guardian of person with disability, verify that guardian is appointed by court, designated authority, or local level committee under applicable law.

title: Exemptions from child provisions
description: Provisions of sections 9(1) and 9(3) not applicable to classes of Data Fiduciaries and purposes in Fourth Schedule, subject to conditions.

title: Annual DPIA and audit for Significant Data Fiduciary
description: Undertake Data Protection Impact Assessment and audit once every 12 months from notification as Significant Data Fiduciary.
deadline: Once in every 12 months from notification

title: Report DPIA and audit to Board
description: Cause the person carrying out DPIA and audit to furnish to Board a report with significant observations.

title: Due diligence on algorithmic software
description: Observe due diligence to verify that technical measures including algorithmic software are not likely to pose risk to rights of Data Principals.

title: Restriction on cross-border transfer of specified personal data
description: Ensure personal data specified by Central Government based on committee recommendations is processed subject to restriction that it is not transferred outside India.
deadline: Upon notification of specified personal data

title: Publish means for exercising rights
description: Prominently publish on website/app details of means for Data Principal to make requests for rights, and particulars required (e.g., username).

title: Grievance redressal within 90 days
description: Prominently publish a grievance redressal system and respond to grievances within a reasonable period not exceeding 90 days; implement appropriate technical and organizational measures.

title: Cross-border transfer restrictions
description: Personal data may be transferred outside India subject to restrictions specified by Central Government regarding making personal data available to foreign State or entities under its control.
deadline: Upon enforcement of applicable Central Government order

title: Exemption for research, archiving, statistical purposes
description: Processing necessary for research, archiving, or statistical purposes exempt from Act if carried on in accordance with Second Schedule standards.

title: Furnish information to Central Government
description: Data Fiduciary or intermediary must furnish information as called for by Central Government for purposes specified in Seventh Schedule, within specified period.
deadline: As specified in the call for information
notes: If disclosure prejudicially affects sovereignty, Data Fiduciary must not disclose to Data Principal without permission.
